chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – The supplication against the enemy

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/177)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/177)
text: إِذَا دَعَا أَحَدُكُمْ عَلَى أَحَدٍ قَالَ.
اللَّهُمَّ اطْرُقْهُ بِبَلِيَّةٍ لَا أُخْتَ لَهَا وَ أَبِحْ حَرِيمَهُ

en.hubeali: And it is reported from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever one of you supplicates against anyone, he should say,
‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Strike him with an affliction there being no comparison for it, and crack his sanctity’.
en.sarwar: 2. It is narrated from abu al-Hassan (a.s.) who has said the following: “When any of you prays against the other person he should say, ‘O Lord, strike him with peerless misfortune in the night and allow his defenses to be ransacked.’”
